scenario: A team's workflow: draft a BPMN in Web Modeler → review → publish → deploy to cluster. They wonder if Web Modeler has explicit lifecycle states for resources.
question: Does Web Modeler distinguish "draft / published / deployed" resource states?

options.text: 7); deployment is a separate action that pushes the current saved content to a Zeebe cluster as a process-definition version on that cluster. md:30-39, where each promotion to a higher stage requires explicit selection (no auto-advance) and Production deploys are restricted to administrators/org-owners. Versions can be compared (visual or XML diff) and restored.

Web Modeler maintains clear distinctions between resource lifecycle states. Auto-save preserves the current draft as you edit. Explicitly created versions (immutable snapshots) record significant checkpoints. Deployment is a distinct action that pushes the content to a Zeebe cluster and registers a new process definition version there. Claiming every save is final misunderstands both the draft-versus-version distinction and the separate deployment step that makes a resource available for execution.

Web Modeler distinguishes more than just a "saved" state. Beyond the auto-saved draft, it supports explicitly created immutable version snapshots that can be compared with visual or XML diffs. The deployment step is entirely separate from saving -- a deployed resource has been pushed to a Zeebe cluster and is available for execution, whereas a saved draft exists only in Web Modeler. 8 add a four-stage deployment pipeline with explicit promotion gates between Development, Testing, Staging, and Production.
The basic draft/saved/deployed lifecycle states are standard in all Web Modeler tiers without requiring project-level configuration. 8 for teams that want formalised promotion gates.

a.text: Correct. Web Modeler distinguishes (per versions.md + process-application-pipeline.md): Draft / saved state: the current editable content; auto-saved as you type/drag; preserved across sessions; collaboration sync via real-time updates. Version (snapshot): created manually via Versions > Create version, or auto-created on drag-and-drop file replace; immutable; can be restored or compared visually/XML. Deployed state (per cluster): at any moment, the modeler clicks Deploy -- the current saved content uploads to a target Zeebe cluster, getting a cluster-side process-definition version. Each deployment is a snapshot; subsequent edits don't auto-update the deployed version. Process application deployment pipeline (8.8+): optional formal pipeline with four stages -- Development → Testing → Staging → Production. The next stage is NOT auto-selected on deploy; users explicitly promote. Production deploys gated by Administrator / Organization Owner role per :39, :53. Practical workflow without process applications: draft → save (auto) → create version (snapshot) → deploy to dev cluster → test → deploy to staging → deploy to prod. With process applications: same plus formal stage promotion.

d.text: Incorrect. Customisable per tier; basic states standard.
